description:
You are assisting a data-scientist who is about to start cleaning some legacy
    CSV datasets stored under

        /home/user/projects/data_cleaning/raw_datasets/

    The scientist wants a reproducible, verifiable back-up before any changes
    occur.  Perform the back-up and create a concise audit log.

    WHAT YOU HAVE RIGHT NOW
    -----------------------
    • /home/user/projects/data_cleaning/raw_datasets/       (directory)
        ├── sales_q1.csv
        ├── sales_q2.csv
        └── customers.csv

    WHAT MUST EXIST WHEN YOU ARE DONE
    ---------------------------------
    1. Archive file
       • Full path: /home/user/archive/raw_datasets_backup.tar.gz
       • It must contain the COMPLETE directory
         /home/user/projects/data_cleaning/raw_datasets/
         (the directory itself and all files inside it),
         keeping the original path structure inside the
         tarball.  Compression must be gzip (.tar.gz).

    2. Log file
       • Full path: /home/user/archive/backup_log.txt
       • Exactly THREE non-empty lines, in this order:

         Line 1: Timestamp of when you created the archive
                 in extended ISO-8601 format
                 YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ss±hh:mm
                 Example: 2024-03-17T14:52:01+00:00

         Line 2: The 64-character hexadecimal SHA-256 checksum
                 of raw_datasets_backup.tar.gz
                 (just the hex string, no filename or
                 “-” afterwards).

         Line 3: A single integer representing the number of
                 regular files that were inside the archived
                 raw_datasets directory (do **not** count
                 sub-directories).  With the initial data,
                 the value must be 3.

       • A trailing newline is allowed but not required.

    3. Clean workspace
       • After successful archiving, the original directory
         /home/user/projects/data_cleaning/raw_datasets/
         must no longer exist (the scientist wants a clean
         slate).  Deleting it is acceptable.

    OPERATIONAL CONSTRAINTS
    -----------------------
    • Work only with standard Linux command-line utilities
      that are present in a minimal distribution
      (tar, gzip, sha256sum, date, wc, etc.).
    • Do NOT request or use elevated (root) privileges.
    • The entire task can be completed in approximately
      5-10 shell commands; keep it simple.
    • Make sure /home/user/archive/ exists (create it if
      necessary) and is writable by the current user.
    • Ensure that the log file adheres EXACTLY to the format
      described above; automated tests will parse each line.

    HINTS (not mandatory)
    ---------------------
    • Use “tar -czf …” (or equivalent) to create the archive.
    • Use “sha256sum” piped through “cut -d' ' -f1” (or
      similar) to isolate the raw hash for line 2.
    • Count the regular files with “find … -type f | wc -l”.
    • The current time can be obtained in the required
      ISO-8601 format with
         date --iso-8601=seconds
      (GNU coreutils).

    DELIVERABLES
    ------------
    Once finished, the filesystem should reflect the three
    results listed above.  The automated grader will
    1) assert that /home/user/archive/raw_datasets_backup.tar.gz
       exists and is a valid gzip archive,
    2) parse /home/user/archive/backup_log.txt and verify:
         • line 1 matches ISO-8601 pattern,
         • line 2 is exactly 64 hex characters,
         • line 3 is the integer “3”,
    3) confirm that
       /home/user/projects/data_cleaning/raw_datasets/
       is no longer present.
truth:
Initial filesystem (before agent execution):

    /home/user/projects/data_cleaning/raw_datasets/
        sales_q1.csv       (contents: "id,amount\n1,100\n")
        sales_q2.csv       (contents: "id,amount\n2,200\n")
        customers.csv      (contents: "id,name\n1,Alice\n2,Bob\n")

    /home/user/archive/   (directory)   ← may or may not exist

    Expected post-task checks by test suite:

    1. Path /home/user/archive/raw_datasets_backup.tar.gz exists,
       is a gzip file, and when extracted recreates
       ./home/user/projects/data_cleaning/raw_datasets/ with
       the three CSV files whose byte-for-byte contents match
       the originals above.

    2. Path /home/user/archive/backup_log.txt exists and:
       • line 1 matches regex
         ^[0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2}T[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}[+-][0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}\s*$
       • line 2 matches regex
         ^[0-9a-f]{64}\s*$
       • line 3 is exactly:
         3

    3. Directory /home/user/projects/data_cleaning/raw_datasets/
       does NOT exist after completion.

    The test suite will ignore the exact SHA-256 value
    and timestamp—only their format matters—but it will
    verify that the checksum listed in the log matches
    the digest of the archive on disk.

    Permissions: everything is owned by user:user and
    writable by the current user.
